methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
NVision Imaging Technologies is a Series A biotech company that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public equities (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms operating in the same Biotech & Health sector, with particular focus on 5 Series B peers (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, Profluent) valued between $600M and $3.3B.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $20M raised baseline, implying a $40M valuation typical for Series A-to-next-stage progression. Peer-Median anchors to the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $805M (range: $100M–$3.3B), reflecting the wide dispersion common to early-stage biotech. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because NVision's revenue is not disclosed. The public biotech median trades at 7.4x sales, but this multiple cannot be scaled without revenue data.

The two applicable methods generate a triangulated range of $34M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $422M. The wide spread reflects the inherent variance in Series B peer valuations and stage-based progression assumptions. Key uncertainties include technology maturity, clinical/commercial traction relative to peers, and path-to-revenue visibility—none of which are quantified in the available dataset.
